'''''UFC 165: Jones vs. Gustafsson''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event held on September 21, 2013, at the [[Air Canada Centre]] in [[Toronto]], Ontario, Canada.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/05/ufc-165-heads-to-air-canada-centre-in-toronto-on-sept-21|title=UFC 165 heads to Air Canada Centre in Toronto on Sept. 21|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2013-05-14|access-date=2013-05-14|archive-date=2013-06-08|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130608092407/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/05/ufc-165-heads-to-air-canada-centre-in-toronto-on-sept-21|url-status=dead}}</ref> The main event was inducted into the [[UFC Hall of Fame#Fight Wing|UFC Hall of Fame's Fight Wing]] in 2021.

==Background==

The main event featured a [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Jon Jones]] and [[Alexander Gustafsson]].<ref name="Dann Stupp and Matt Erickson">{{cite web|url=http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/06/champ-jon-jones-vs-alexander-gustafsson-to-headline-ufc-165-in-toronto|title=Champ Jon Jones vs. Alexander Gustafsson to headline UFC 165 in Toronto|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Dann Stupp and Matt Erickson|date=2013-06-13|access-date=2013-06-13|archive-date=2013-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130616145723/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/06/champ-jon-jones-vs-alexander-gustafsson-to-headline-ufc-165-in-toronto|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/9384346/jon-jones-set-ufc-title-defense-alexander-gustafsson|title=Jones-Gustafsson set for UFC 165|publisher=espn.go.com.com|author=Franklin McNeil|date=2013-06-13|access-date=2013-06-13}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ufc.com/rankings|title=UFC Fighter Rankings|date=2013-06-17|access-date=2013-06-17}}</ref>  Gustafsson earned his title fight by winning six straight fights in the light heavyweight division.<ref name="Dann Stupp and Matt Erickson"/> Additionally, Jones was looking to surpass the number of Light Heavyweight title defenses originally set by [[Tito Ortiz]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/2013/4/22/4254696/jon-jones-wants-to-break-tito-ortizs-record-before-turning-to-super|title=Jon Jones wants to break Tito Ortiz's record before turning to superfights|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Dave Doyle|date=2013-04-22|access-date=2013-09-25}}</ref>

Co-featured on the card was a rescheduled Interim [[UFC Bantamweight Championship|Bantamweight Championship]] bout between the current champion [[Renan Barão]], and former [[List of WEC champions#Bantamweight Championship|WEC Bantamweight Champion]] and top contender [[Eddie Wineland]].<ref name="titlebout">{{cite web|url=http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/07/renan-barao-eddie-wineland-meet-for-interim-135-title-as-ufc-165-co-main-event|title=Renan Barao, Eddie Wineland meet for interim 135 title as UFC 165 co-main event|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Matt Erickson|date=2013-07-03|access-date=2013-07-03|archive-date=2013-07-06|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130706191527/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/07/renan-barao-eddie-wineland-meet-for-interim-135-title-as-ufc-165-co-main-event|url-status=dead}}</ref>  The pairing was first planned as the headliner at [[UFC 161]], but was removed from that event after Barão injured his foot.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://thestarphoenix.com/sports/mma/Barao+Wineland+interim+title+bout+Toronto/8613503/story.html|title=Barao, Wineland interim title bout set for UFC 165 in Toronto|publisher=thestarphoenix.com|author=Dave Deibert|date=2013-07-03|access-date=2013-07-03|archive-date=2013-09-25|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130925185705/http://www.thestarphoenix.com/sports/mma/Barao+Wineland+interim+title+bout+Toronto/8613503/story.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

[[Mark Bocek]] was expected to face [[Michel Prazeres]] at this event. However, Bocek pulled out due to an injury and was replaced by UFC newcomer [[Jesse Ronson]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/08/newcomer-jesse-ronson-in-for-injured-mark-bocek-vs-michel-prazeres-at-ufc-165|title=Newcomer Jesse Ronson in for injured Mark Bocek vs. Michel Prazeres at UFC 165|publisher=MMAJunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2013-08-22|access-date=2013-08-22|archive-date=2013-08-22|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130822200644/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/08/newcomer-jesse-ronson-in-for-injured-mark-bocek-vs-michel-prazeres-at-ufc-165|url-status=dead}}</ref>

[[Norifumi Yamamoto]] was expected to face [[Ivan Menjivar]] at the event.  However, Yamamoto was removed from the bout and was replaced by [[Wilson Reis]].<ref name="KidOut/ReisIn">{{cite web|url=http://mmadiehards.com/live/features/wilson-reis-replaces-kid-yamamoto-faces-ivan-menjivar-at-ufc-165/|title=Wilson Reis Replaces "Kid" Yamamoto, Faces Ivan Menjivar at UFC 165|publisher=MMAdiehards.com|author=Cameron Gidari|date=2013-09-02|access-date=2013-09-02}}</ref>

The event also aired October 17, 2013, on [[Fox Sports 1]] less than a month after it took place as part of a special UFC Reloaded.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://msn.foxsports.com/ufc/story/jones-gustafsson-airs-on-reloaded-101513|title=Jones-Gustafsson hits UFC Reloaded|work=Foxsports.com|author=Staff|date=2013-10-15|access-date=2013-10-15}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/09/ufc-165-bonuses-jones-gustafsson-barao-gagnon-earn-50000-honors |title=UFC 165 bonuses: Jones, Gustafsson, Barao, Gagnon earn $50,000 honors |publisher=MMAjunkie.com |date=2013-09-22 |access-date=2013-09-22 |author=Staff |archive-date=2013-11-06 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131106201226/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/09/ufc-165-bonuses-jones-gustafsson-barao-gagnon-earn-50000-honors |url-status=dead }}</ref>
* '''Fight of The Night: Jon Jones vs. Alexander Gustafsson'''
* '''Knockout of The Night: Renan Barão'''
* '''Submission of the Night: Mitch Gagnon'''
